audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel / Shemuel Bet chapter 7, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for royal rest, cedar/curtain contrast, Nathan's initial approval and correction, mobile sanctuary, shepherd-to-ruler memory, land and enemy language, house/dynasty wordplay, sonship, human rods, enduring covenant-kindness, forever-throne claims, David's prayer, Israel's election, exodus/conquest rhetoric, and later messianic reception.

verses.restored: When king lived in his house and YHWH had given him rest all around from all his enemies,
king said to Nathan the prophet, See now, I live in house of cedar, while ark of Divine lives within curtains.
Nathan said to king, Go, do all that is in your heart, because YHWH is with you.
In that night, word of YHWH came to Nathan, saying,
Go and say to my servant, to David, Thus says YHWH: Is it you who will build for me a house for my dwelling?
For I have not lived in a house from day I brought children of Israel up from Egypt even to this day, but I have walked about in tent and tabernacle.

In all places where I walked with all children of Israel, did I speak a word with one of tribes/judges of Israel whom I commanded to shepherd my people Israel, saying, Why have you not built for me a house of cedar?
Now thus you shall say to my servant, to David: Thus says YHWH of hosts, I took you from pasture, from after flock, to be leader over my people, over Israel.
I was with you wherever you went, and I cut off all your enemies from before you. I will make for you a great name, like name of great ones who are on earth.
I will appoint a place for my people Israel and plant them, so they may dwell in their place and no longer be disturbed. Children of injustice shall not afflict them again as at beginning,
and from day I appointed judges over my people Israel. I will give you rest from all your enemies. YHWH declares to you that YHWH will make a house for you.
When your days are fulfilled and you lie down with your ancestors, I will raise up your seed after you, who will come out from your body, and I will establish his kingdom.
He will build a house for my Name, and I will establish throne of his kingdom forever.
I will be to him as father, and he will be to me as son. When he commits wrong, I will correct him with rod of humans and with blows of children of humanity.
But my covenant-kindness shall not turn aside from him, as I turned it aside from Saul, whom I removed from before you.
Your house and your kingdom shall be made faithful forever before you; your throne shall be established forever.
According to all these words and according to all this vision, so Nathan spoke to David.
King David came and sat before YHWH and said, Who am I, Lord YHWH, and what is my house, that you have brought me this far?
This was still small in your eyes, Lord YHWH, and you have spoken also concerning house of your servant for a distant time. And this is instruction/law of humanity, Lord YHWH.
What more can David add to speak to you? You know your servant, Lord YHWH.
For sake of your word and according to your heart, you have done all this greatness, making it known to your servant.
Therefore you are great, Lord YHWH, because there is none like you and there is no Divine besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

Who is like your people, like Israel, one nation on earth whom Divine went to redeem as a people for Divine-self, to make a name, and to do for you great and fearsome things for your land before your people whom you redeemed for yourself from Egypt, nations and their gods?
You established for yourself your people Israel as your people forever, and you, YHWH, became their Divine.
Now, YHWH Divine, word that you spoke concerning your servant and concerning his house, establish it forever and do as you have spoken.
May your Name be made great forever, saying, YHWH of hosts is Divine over Israel, and may house of your servant David be established before you.
For you, YHWH of hosts, Divine of Israel, uncovered ear of your servant, saying, I will build a house for you. Therefore your servant has found his heart to pray this prayer to you.
Now, Lord YHWH, you are Divine, your words are truth, and you have spoken this good concerning your servant.
Now be willing and bless house of your servant, that it may be forever before you, because you, Lord YHWH, have spoken, and from your blessing let house of your servant be blessed forever.

translation_notes: Nathan first approves David's plan and then receives a corrective word that night. Prophetic counsel can require revision; initial confidence is not infallibility.
YHWH's mobile tent presence resists the idea that Divine reality depends upon royal architecture, centralized institutions, or expensive buildings.
The Hebrew word 'house' shifts among palace, temple, household, and dynasty. The literary promise should not be flattened into a single political slogan.
The promise arises within Israel's covenantal scripture. Christian messianic readings may not erase Jewish interpretation, claim replacement of Israel, or weaponize the text against Judaism.
An enduring Davidic house does not create universal hereditary monarchy, Divine right of kings, nationalism, theocracy, or immunity for political families.
The father-son and rod language describes ancient royal correction. It may not justify corporal punishment, child abuse, state violence, or the claim that suffering proves Divine discipline.
Promises of planting Israel and cutting enemies use settlement and conquest language. They must not become modern territorial entitlement or erasure of other peoples.
Israel's election is relational vocation, not biological supremacy. The exodus memory may not support ethnic hierarchy or antisemitic supersessionism.
David's prayer receives the promise with humility, yet it also embraces perpetual dynasty. Gratitude does not remove the need to audit how royal houses consolidate power.
The repeated 'forever' language carries theological, literary, and historical tension given later exile and monarchy's end; that tension should remain open.
safety_note: Maximum anti-theocratic and anti-supersessionist safeguards: Do not turn the Davidic covenant into hereditary leader immunity, Christian replacement of Israel, modern land entitlement, corporal-punishment theology, or sacred architecture fundraising pressure. Divine presence precedes palace and temple, and covenant never cancels accountability.
